text: The patient is positioned bilaterally for a laparoscopic splenectomy and resection of the splenic artery aneurysm. A 15mm trocar is placed in the left midclavicular line, and a 5mm trocar is inserted in the epigastrium. The gravid uterus is visible, and desufflation of the left flank is performed to facilitate the insertion of the 5mm trocar by the assistant.

The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to divide the attachments of the lower pole of the spleen, including the colosplenic, ferrocolic, and ferrous splenic ligaments. The spleen is mobilized and freed from the ventral surface of the left kidney. A 4x4 gauze is introduced for anterior retraction of the left lobe of the liver. The gastro splenic ligament is exposed, and the assistant provides lateral retraction of the spleen while the choro gastric vessels are taken down with the harmonic scalpel. The spleen is finally freed from its attachments.

The surgeon identifies the splenic artery and begins dissection using a harmonic scalpel, moving proximally with a hook artery. The splenic artery is isolated from the tail of the pancreas. A small accessory splenic artery is identified, clipped, and divided with scissors. A window is created using the harmonic scalpel.
The surgeon uses two graspers to lift the spleen, exposing the splenic vein. The aneurysm is visible above the spleen.
